name: Serra de Montejunto Loop
name_original: Carreiro da Senhora — percurso circular na Serra de Montejunto
description: Hike the difficult Carreiro da Senhora circular route in Serra de Montejunto, covering 11.1 miles with 2174 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Cabanas De Torres offers access to diverse natural landscapes, primarily centered around the Serra de Montejunto. This region in western Portugal features varied terrain, including mountainous areas with significant elevation changes and potential karstic formations. The trails often provide scenic views and wind through natural settings, suitable for different levels of hiking experience. Best hiking trails around Cabanas De Torres The most popular hiking route is Route of the Windmills, a 6.4 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a notable elevation gain of over 450 meters. Another top favourite among local hikers is Trilho da Quinta da Serra — circular route in Serra de Montejunto, a moderate 3.0 miles (4.9 km) path. This trail offers a circular experience within the Serra de Montejunto, with a more gentle elevation profile. Local hikers also love the Trail of the Carreiro dos SS — circular route in the Serra de Montejunto, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Serra de Montejunto, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Cabanas De Torres is defined by the mountainous terrain of Serra de Montejunto, offering both challenging ascents and more gentle circular routes. The network provides options for various ability levels, from easy walks to difficult trails. The routes in Cabanas De Torres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 280 hikers have used komoot to explore Cabanas De Torres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cabanas De Torres?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Cabanas De Torres, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes primarily explore the diverse landscapes of the Serra de Montejunto.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for hiking in Cabanas De Torres?
A: The terrain around Cabanas De Torres is largely defined by the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Montejunto&lt;/strong&gt;, featuring mountainous areas with varied elevation changes. You can expect paths winding through natural settings, and some areas may include karstic formations, offering diverse landscapes from easy walks to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there hiking options for different skill levels in Cabanas De Torres?
A: Yes, Cabanas De Torres offers a good range of trails for various abilities. You'll find 6 easy routes for a relaxed stroll, 16 moderate trails for a balanced challenge, and 4 difficult routes for experienced hikers seeking more demanding ascents within the Serra de Montejunto.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see on hikes near Cabanas De Torres?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting natural and man-made landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Montejunto Watchtower&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/760297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt; provide a unique historical sight. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ota River&lt;/a&gt; and various natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel Bend&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cabanas De Torres?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380477485/trilho-da-quinta-da-serra-circular-route-in-serra-de-montejunto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trilho da Quinta da Serra — circular route in Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.9 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380484809/trail-of-the-carreiro-dos-ss-circular-route-in-the-serra-de-montejunto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Carreiro dos SS — circular route in the Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt; offers an 13.7 km circular experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cabanas De Torres?
A: The hiking trails around Cabanas De Torres are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic views from the Serra de Montejunto, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families near Cabanas De Torres?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails perfect for families. The region offers paths that are less demanding, allowing for a pleasant outdoor experience. An example of an easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10344128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Montejunto Bar loop from Paisagem Protegida da Serra de Montejunto&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 3.5 km hike.

Q: Can I find longer, more challenging hikes in the Cabanas De Torres area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a greater challenge, the region offers longer and more difficult routes, primarily within the Serra de Montejunto. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380551821/serra-de-montejunto-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Montejunto Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.9 km trail with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any lakes or water features to see while hiking?
A: While hiking, you might encounter various water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ota River&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural feature. Additionally, there are several lakes in the broader area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7152796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoa de Alcoentre&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5869247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeço de Meca Quarry Lake&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cabanas De Torres?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, Portugal generally offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially in mountainous areas, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if hiking during this season.

Q: Are there any trails that offer significant elevation changes or panoramic views?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of the Serra de Montejunto provides routes with significant elevation changes and opportunities for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380443753/route-of-the-windmills" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Windmills&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features an elevation gain of over 450 meters, leading to rewarding vistas.
tips: Very pleasant path that runs around Montejunto. It offers beautiful landscapes of the region. Some sections have loose stone floors, so it is worth paying attention.

At the top of the Montejunto mountain range, very close to the chapel of Nossa Senhora das Neves, there are remains of the first convent of the Order of São Domingos in Portugal. Initially built in 1218, shortly after Friar Soeiro Gomes arrived in Portugal, the current ruins are the result of the rebuilding (over the initial convent) that took place in the 18th century, upon the return of the Dominicans. Despite being in ruins, it is a grandiose monument that must be visited, with due care, given the dangers that ruins always contain.

Source: Natural.pt

Next to the monument, there is a trail called "Calçada dos Frades" that runs along the slope. This cobbled path, which is also thought to be from the 18th century, was opened to serve the construction and use of the Convent of Nossa Senhora das Neves. The path was also used by the friars to access the Real Fabrica do Gelo, located further down, where today there is a laser area and an interpretive center.

Located in the heart of the Montejunto mountain range, the Montejunto Rural Camping Park has ample shade and capacity for 90 people. Free to use, it has a bar, a tourist reception center and an Environmental Interpretation Centre.

https://montejunto.pt/parque-de-campismo-rural-da-serra-de-montejunto/

Located in the chestnut woods, it connects the Real Fábrica do Gelo complex and the Environmental Interpretation Centre. The Park has eight tables, with an average capacity of six to eight people per table. As support structures for its use, it has a small bar, toilets and a small playground.

Magnificently located in the Serra do Montejunto, famous for having the largest concentration of windmills in the Iberian Peninsula, Moinho de Avis is an absolutely unmissable windmill that dates back to 1810.

This typical windmill, with its clay pots set with sails, is located next to the Curral do Burro restaurant, providing the flour that is then used to make fresh bread, from the rare Barbela wheat.

Miguel Nobre, a carpenter by profession, is also a huge millology enthusiast and was responsible for the restoration of the Avis Mill in 2008. Restoring mills quickly became a full-time job.

Moinho de Avis is the largest windmill in Portugal.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The Carreiro da Senhora route is considered difficult due to its significant elevation gain, steep climbs, and winding paths. You'll traverse a varied limestone landscape, part of the Maciço Calcário Estremenho, featuring rocky sections, imposing crags, and deep valleys. Sure-footedness is essential, and the surface can be hard-packed dirt or rocks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Carreiro da Senhora trail?
faqs.answer: Many trails in Serra de Montejunto, including variations of this route, are accessible from or near the Environmental Interpretation Center of the Serra de Montejunto Protected Landscape. This is a good starting point to look for parking options.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Carreiro da Senhora route?
faqs.answer: While the Serra de Montejunto is a protected natural area, dogs are generally allowed on trails. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for encounters with other hikers or animals.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Serra de Montejunto offers a pleasant hiking experience in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant. In spring, you'll see wildflowers and aromatic flora like thyme and rosemary. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended, while winter can bring cooler, potentially wet conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in the Serra de Montejunto Protected Landscape?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Carreiro da Senhora route or other trails within the Paisagem Protegida da Serra de Montejunto. It's a publicly accessible natural area, but always respect local regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers breathtaking panoramic views, including vistas over the Atlantic Ocean on clear days. You'll pass by the ruins of the Chapel of Our Lady of the Snows (Ruins), a significant cultural landmark. Other highlights include the Meia Encosta Trail and the historic Moinho de Avis.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Carreiro da Senhora route intersects with and utilizes sections of several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the [CIMA PR06] Montejunto * N-115, [CIMA PR06] Montejunto * Vilar, and [CIMA PR06] Montejunto * Pragança. It also crosses the historic Calçada dos Frades and sections of the Trilho da Casa do Guarda.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plant life can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Serra de Montejunto is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species and even deer. The plant life shifts from Mediterranean scrubland to oak forests, with aromatic flora like thyme and rosemary, and a display of wildflowers in the spring.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like picnic areas or campsites along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by the Picnic Area of Serra de Montejunto, which can be a good spot for a break. For those looking to stay overnight, the Montejunto Rural Campsite is also located nearby.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Carreiro da Senhora route?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating and varied terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate layers of clothing. A map or GPS device is also recommended, as some sections can be challenging to navigate. Trekking poles can be very helpful on steep or rocky sections.

waypoints.name: Chapel of Our Lady of the Snows (Ruins)
Picnic Area of Serra de Montejunto
